content.problemStatement:
You are organizing a charity event where people donate various items, and you want to
keep track of the number of donated items of each type. You use an array called
donationCounts to represent the number of donations of each type, where the value at
each index corresponds to the count of donations for that type.
However, sometimes multiple people donate the same type of item simultaneously,
leading to duplicate counts in the array. To rectify this, you decide to increment the
count of each duplicate item in the array by 1 until all counts become unique. Each
increment operation corresponds to a "move."
You are given an integer array donationCounts. In one move, you can pick an index i
where 0 <= i < donationCounts.length and increment donationCounts[i] by 1.
Your task is to determine the minimum number of moves needed to ensure that each
type of donated item has a unique count in the donationCounts array.
